title: Modules, Packages, and Dependency Boundaries · Semantics Case: Module Graph
summary: Split the status coordinator into explicit browser modules and verify clean-environment loading and dependency behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the js module graph increment in a modular status application with a dependency record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace static module graph linking, live bindings, execution order, and cyclic dependency risks.
Use named, default, namespace, side-effect, and re-export forms with explicit public boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
